description: Canonical requirements registry mapping requirements to test cases, source modules, and issues. Generated from existing test structure, locked modules, and architecture docs. See ADR-0002 for design rationale. Updated with ITM Sections A-K (29 new requirements).

requirements.title: Module lock gate prevents unauthorized changes to critical modules
requirements.description: The lock gate enforces that changes to locked modules (registered in .module_lock_registry.json) require explicit exception approval via lock_gate_exceptions.json. The gate itself, its registry, and its CI workflow must also be locked to prevent tampering.

requirements.title: Impact Gate validates fix/bug issues before transition to done
requirements.description: The Impact Gate runs FR acceptance tests and bug regression tests when fix/bug issues transition in_review → done. Must verify at minimum 10 tests pass before allowing the transition. Issues with impact-gate-bypass label skip the gate.

requirements.title: Blast Radius worker detects impact of code changes and posts reports on issues
requirements.description: When fix/bug issues transition to in_review, the Blast Radius worker queries the Touch Index to determine which FRs and bugs share overlapping files with the changed code. It posts a structured markdown report on the issue and @-mentions impacted FR owners.

requirements.title: Touch Index maintains file-to-issue mapping for FRs and bugs
requirements.description: The Touch Index system ingests issue data from Paperclip, extracts file paths from comments, git history, and issue descriptions, and maintains PostgreSQL tables (touch_index_fr_files, touch_index_bug_files) as the canonical file-to-issue mapping for blast radius and impact gate queries.

requirements.title: Lock gate exception entries must be validated against schema and authority model
requirements.description: Lock gate exceptions must follow the board-approved authority model: planned changes approved by board (unlimited duration), emergency changes approved by CEO (4-hour max). CTO no longer has unilateral unlock authority. All entries must reference valid approval IDs and authorized approvers.

requirements.title: Canary trade execution test must always pass — detects zero-trades regression
requirements.description: The canary trade execution test loads real OHLCV data, runs the full signal-evaluator-to-trade-registry chain, and asserts total_trades > 0. This catches the zero-trades failure class within 5 minutes of CI rather than 10 hours after QA sign-off.

requirements.title: Dependency graph maps file-level imports and is regenerated on schedule
requirements.description: The dependency graph (dep_graph.json) provides forward and reverse file dependency mappings used by blast radius and impact analysis. Must be regenerated on a schedule to stay current with the codebase.

requirements.title: Nightly test alert notifies on scheduled test failures
requirements.description: When the nightly test suite fails, a Paperclip alert must be created with the workflow name, run URL, and test output. This ensures test failures are surfaced to the team within the same day.

requirements.title: scan-done retroactively gates fix/bug issues that bypassed the Impact Gate
requirements.description: The scan-done worker retroactively validates fix/bug issues already in done status that never passed through the Impact Gate. It backfills coverage for issues completed before the gate existed or that slipped through.

requirements.title: UnifiedDataManager serves as single source of truth for all data access
requirements.description: The UnifiedDataManager provides a singleton interface for data ingestion, caching, and retrieval across all subsystems (backtesting, live trading, dry-run). Any uncoordinated change risks cascading failures across all pipelines.

requirements.title: Data quality validation gate enforces minimum quality thresholds before pipeline execution
requirements.description: Before any backtest or live trading pipeline executes, data quality must be validated against minimum thresholds for completeness, accuracy, and freshness. Sub-threshold data blocks pipeline execution until remediated.

requirements.title: Database layer under optimizer_v3/database provides unified ORM access for 9 managers
requirements.description: The optimizer_v3/database package contains 9 ORM managers providing unified database access for strategy configs, trade results, and calibration state. Uncoordinated schema or connection changes risk data corruption across all tools.

requirements.title: ITM state manager maintains live position persistence for institutional trade management
requirements.description: The ITM state manager is the persistence layer for live positions. Accidental changes can cause incorrect P&L tracking or position reconciliation failures. Must maintain data integrity across restarts and handle concurrent access.

requirements.title: PerformanceMonitor auto-pauses strategies when drawdown or daily loss exceeds thresholds
requirements.description: System MUST auto-pause an ACTIVE strategy when drawdown or daily loss exceeds configured thresholds under performance monitoring. This is a real-money safety requirement (FDR-850).

requirements.title: Core domain entities define Instrument, Order, Position, Signal, Decision objects for ITM
requirements.description: The ITM domain model provides strongly-typed dataclass entities (Instrument, Order, Position, Signal, Decision, RiskProfile, AccountHeat, CapitalState) that are pure Python with no NautilusTrader or I/O dependencies. Domain integrity via post_init validation across all entities. Decimal precision for all monetary values. Immutable value objects where practical.

requirements.title: Versioned configuration system with environment separation and runtime reload
requirements.description: ITMConfig provides a frozen validated configuration model with Environment enum (dev/test/prod). Config loader merges layered sources: environment defaults -> YAML file -> base config dict -> env vars -> runtime overrides. Config carries monotonically increasing revision numbers for stale reference detection. All quantitative thresholds loaded from versioned artifacts.

requirements.title: NautilusTrader mapping utilities bridge ITM domain and NT concepts
requirements.description: The ITM domain model maps to NautilusTrader concepts via nt_mapping module, providing clean conversion between ITM domain objects (Instrument, Order, Position) and NautilusTrader equivalents. Ensures compatibility with NT strategy/actor APIs without coupling ITM domain to NT types.

requirements.title: Binance WebSocket client subscribes to real-time market data streams
requirements.description: The Binance client provides real-time WebSocket connectivity to Binance for BTCUSDT perpetual futures. Must handle connection lifecycle, message parsing, rate limit compliance, and reconnection with exponential backoff. Transforms raw WebSocket messages to normalized bar series.

requirements.title: Historical data gap detection and LakeAPI backfill
requirements.description: ITM must detect missing or stale bars across 1m and 15m windows and trigger historical data fetch via external provider (LakeAPI or equivalent). Gap detection validates timestamp consistency, monotonicity, and no duplicates. Merged real-time and historical data must be consistent.

requirements.title: Data freshness validation enforces no-trade gating on stale data
requirements.description: All data used for ITM decisions must be <60 seconds old. If data is stale or missing, the system must flag data as invalid and propagate a no-trade gating signal to downstream consumers. Data freshness envelope must be computed before any decision cycle.

requirements.title: State checkpointing with crash-safe persistence to PostgreSQL and Redis
requirements.description: The ITM state manager provides periodic checkpointing of positions, orders, risk metrics, strategy states, and event history markers to dual stores (PostgreSQL and Redis). Checkpointing must complete <500ms and be crash-safe: either last checkpoint is fully valid or transactionally rolled back.

requirements.title: Recovery framework restores ITM state within sub-2-minute targets
requirements.description: On startup or after outage, the recovery framework reloads the last consistent checkpoint, rebuilds in-memory state from persisted stores, and re-syncs market data. Recovery targets: <=2 minutes for <=24h outage, <=5 minutes for <=7-day outage. Must handle clean shutdown, sudden outage with partial checkpoints, and long outage scenarios.

requirements.title: State schema versioning supports domain model migrations
requirements.description: Persisted state must be versioned and compatible with domain model versioning from Section A. State schema defines versioned serialization format. Migration hooks support upgrades when domain schemas change. Must handle persistence using older schema versions with successful upgrade to newer schemas.

requirements.title: Strategy registry enables dynamic loading, enable/disable, and lifecycle management
requirements.description: The StrategyRegistry provides runtime loading of strategies from configuration, enabling/disabling without ITM restart, and lifecycle tracking. Strategies are loaded via the SBExportImporter contract from Strategy Builder exports, with validation of institutional hard limits (max leverage=1.0, max position qty, max daily loss).

requirements.title: Signal aggregator collects, normalizes, and routes signals from active strategies
requirements.description: The SignalAggregator collects outputs from all active strategies each 15-minute decision cycle, normalizes and timestamps signals, and routes them to the confluence engine. Must handle misconfigured or failing strategies gracefully. Signals carry direction, strength (0-1), source strategy, and expiry metadata.

requirements.title: Capital allocator distributes governance capital across active strategies
requirements.description: The CapitalAllocator manages distribution of governance capital across strategies according to their capital_allocation_pct. Must enforce that total allocation across all strategies <= 100%. Supports runtime rebalancing when strategies are enabled/disabled. Allocation fed from SB export contract.

requirements.title: 4-layer ML ensemble integrates TCN, LSTM-Transformer, LightGBM, and anomaly detection
requirements.description: The ML ensemble provides probabilistic forecasts and confidence scores from four layers: TCN, LSTM-Transformer, LightGBM meta-learner, and anomaly detector. Inputs are normalized time series features from market data plus strategy signals from Section D. Ensemble contributes a configurable weight (default 40%) to final ITM decision score.

requirements.title: SHAP-based explainability gate validates economic sense of predictions
requirements.description: SHAP feature attributions validate the economic sense of ensemble predictions. If explanations do not align with configured patterns or thresholds, ensemble output must be gated or reduced in weight. Stale signals or missing features incur explicit penalties. Gate must be callable within 15-minute cycle deadline (T+14:55).

requirements.title: Risk gate enforces per-trade and portfolio-level risk constraints with fail-safe defaults
requirements.description: The RiskGate enforces all risk governance: per-trade risk limits, max concurrent risk, max drawdown per day/week, account heat thresholds. Must default to non-trading on missing data or inconsistent state. Risk parameters loaded from versioned ITMConfig (RiskParams sub-config). Strictly non-bypassable.

requirements.title: Capital governor tracks governance capital vs exchange balance with limit enforcement
requirements.description: The CapitalGovernor tracks governance capital vs exchange balance (which can differ). All trade sizing must use governance capital as reference. Capital thresholds for halting or reducing risk are enforced (e.g., after specific drawdown). Metrics tracked via CapitalMetrics. Behavior when exchange balance deviates from governance capital must be deterministic.

requirements.title: Emergency closeout and fail-safe non-trading defaults protect against system uncertainty
requirements.description: The EmergencyCloseout handler provides emergency position flattening and risk-reducing actions when system uncertainty or critical thresholds are breached. On missing data, inconsistent state, or configuration failures, ITM must default to non-trading / risk-reducing actions. Must not escalate losses during failure scenarios.

requirements.title: Execution engine translates ITM decisions to concrete orders with TP/SL and DCA
requirements.description: The ExecutionEngine converts ITM Decision objects into executable orders via OrderFactory. Supports Market, Limit, Stop-Market, TP/SL, and DCA order types compatible with Binance. BracketManager handles linked TP/SL order lifecycle. TWAP logic for larger orders within allowed time windows. Limit price optimization based on order book microstructure.

requirements.title: Order state machine tracks full order lifecycle from submission to terminal state
requirements.description: The OrderStateMachine tracks each order through its lifecycle: pending, open, partial, closed, cancelled, error. Manages transitions based on exchange events and internal state changes. Persists order execution events for audit trail. Must correctly handle partial fills, cancellations, rejections, and timeouts. Enforces correct transition ordering.

requirements.title: Rate limiter enforces Binance API rate limits with safe retry and backoff
requirements.description: The RateLimiter enforces Binance API rate limits across all order management and data query endpoints. Uses token bucket algorithm with configurable rates. Implements safe retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures. Must prevent rate-limit ban while maintaining trading throughput.

requirements.title: Position verifier confirms position close on exchange before capital state updates
requirements.description: The PositionVerifier confirms each position close on Binance within 30 seconds by querying NautilusTrader portfolio/account APIs or Binance REST directly. Continuous monitoring of positions every 30-60 seconds for discrepancies. Only after successful verification may capital and risk state be updated for that position.

requirements.title: Discrepancy detection triggers corrective actions when ITM and exchange positions diverge
requirements.description: If ITM believes a position is closed but Binance shows an open position, the PositionVerifier must trigger corrective logic such as emergency flatten or alert. Record all discrepancies for audit and analysis. Must handle: ITM closed but exchange open, exchange partially closed, data mismatch, and transient API errors.

requirements.title: Structured event logging with correlation IDs across decision-execution-PnL chain
requirements.description: The ITM event system provides structured logging of all decisions, signals, orders, executions, and risk events. Events carry correlation IDs linking decision -> orders -> executions -> verification -> PnL. Uses typed domain events for machine processing. Enables full audit trail reconstruction for any trade.

requirements.title: Performance Monitor tracks trading and risk metrics across all strategies
requirements.description: The PerformanceMonitor tracks per-strategy and system-level metrics: Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, profit factor, win rate, account heat utilization, latency, and error rates. Exposes metric data for Section I monitoring. Must persist metrics for historical comparison. Also enforces auto-pause on configured thresholds (overlaps with REQ-ITM-002).

requirements.title: Monitoring integration points for Prometheus/Grafana dashboarding and alerting
requirements.description: ITM must expose integration points for external monitoring systems (Prometheus/Grafana or equivalent). Alerting thresholds on critical metrics: drawdown, error rate, latency breaches, data freshness. System-level metrics: latency, throughput, queue sizes. Trading metrics accessible via structured output.

requirements.title: ITM Orchestrator composes all subsystems into unified 15-minute decision cycle
requirements.description: The ITM Orchestrator integrates all subsystems: consumes market data from data layer, strategy signals from Section D, ensemble outputs from Section E, risk constraints from Section F, and state services from Section C. Executes full decision cycle every 15 minutes. Final decision must be ready by T+14:55 for each bar. Manages actor composition within NautilusTrader event model.

requirements.title: Dry run / paper trading mode validates ITM decisions without real orders
requirements.description: The dry run subsystem provides paper trading execution where orders are simulated rather than sent to the exchange. Includes a runner for simulated execution, a monitor that validates decision quality, and a report generator for dry-run performance analysis. Must match real execution logic exactly, only differing in exchange submission.

requirements.title: Integration test suite covers normal, volatile, data-failure, exchange-failure, and recovery scenarios
requirements.description: The final integration test suite must cover: Normal market conditions (trending and range-bound), high volatility (sudden spikes and regime shifts), data issues (stale data, provider failure, corrupted data), exchange issues (order rejection, partial fills, verification failure), state recovery (short and long outages), ML edge cases (model drift, missing features), and risk edge cases (max drawdown, account heat breach).

requirements.title: Integration test sign-off criteria define performance, risk, data, verification, and explainability SLAs
requirements.description: ITM must satisfy all sign-off criteria: End-to-end decision latency <350ms per cycle, data validation <50ms per bar, checkpointing <500ms. No breach of configured risk limits. No trades executed on stale/corrupted data. Position close verification >99.9%. Recovery tests meet <=2min and <=5min targets. SHAP explainability gates active. All executed trades have traceable rationales.

requirements.title: Kill switch provides emergency stop for all trading activity
requirements.description: The ITM must support a kill switch mechanism that immediately halts all trading activity, cancels pending orders, and prevents new order submission. The kill switch must be triggerable programmatically and via monitoring alerts. Recovery from kill switch requires explicit re-enable after state verification.

requirements.title: QA fact-check pipeline validates strategic claims made by AI consultant
requirements.description: The QA fact-check pipeline runs automated verification of claims made by the AI Consultant. It validates factual accuracy against data and issue history, producing verdict reports (PASS/FAIL) with evidence.

requirements.title: Locked module exception requests must include 4-item QA sign-off checklist
requirements.description: All locked module exception requests must include a mandatory QA sign-off checklist covering: (1) test coverage verification, (2) regression risk assessment, (3) rollback plan, (4) impacted FR identification. This is enforced via the issue template.

requirements.title: InstitutionalSignalEvaluator evaluates and ranks institutional-grade signals before optimizer entry
requirements.description: The InstitutionalSignalEvaluator is the gate that evaluates and ranks all institutional-grade signals before they enter the optimizer. Changes here can alter portfolio allocation logic across all modes. Must include signal health telemetry and canary checks to detect zero-trades scenarios.

requirements.title: Building block registry dynamically discovers and registers all detector building block types
requirements.description: The BlockRegistry provides dynamic discovery and registration of all detector building block types. Uncoordinated changes can break detector instantiation across the entire pipeline. Must support graceful degradation when individual blocks fail to instantiate.

requirements.title: All 83 building block indicators must instantiate and produce valid signals
requirements.description: The system contains 83 building block indicators (adaptive_momentum_oscillator through wyckoff_reaccumulation). Each must instantiate correctly and produce valid signal outputs when provided with market data. Failures in any block can cause silent signal loss and zero-trade scenarios.

requirements.title: Exit conditions must reference signals that exist in the strategy config
requirements.description: Exit conditions that reference non-existent signals cause silent failures in the exit evaluation pipeline. The system must validate exit signal references at strategy config time and reject or warn on missing references.

requirements.title: StrategyValidator enforces NautilusTrader-compatible strategy quality standards
requirements.description: The StrategyValidator is the core validation engine that enforces quality standards for strategies before they are submitted for backtesting or live trading. Uncoordinated changes can silently pass invalid strategies into live trading and optimizer pipelines.

requirements.title: Auto-Fix Safety Framework applies corrective modifications with rollback capability
requirements.description: The Auto-Fix Safety Framework is a real-money protection layer that applies corrective modifications to strategies. Includes UndoManager for one-level-deep undo support. All auto-fixes must be safe, reversible, and verified before application.

requirements.title: Backtest configuration panel wiring must produce different results for different parameter values
requirements.description: All backtest configuration parameters in the UI must be correctly wired to the backtest engine. Changing a parameter value (date range, capital, signal thresholds) must produce measurably different backtest results. Identical results indicate a wiring bug.

requirements.title: Strategy Builder export contract defines JSON/YAML interface for ITM strategy import
requirements.description: The SBExportImporter defines and validates the JSON/YAML contract between the Strategy Builder and ITM. Supports versioned export format (sb_export_version '1.0'). Validates per-strategy institutional hard limits: max_leverage=1.0, max_position_qty<=1.0 BTC, max_daily_loss<=500 USDT. Enforces total capital allocation <= 100% across all strategies in a single export.

requirements.title: Multi-strategy signal aggregation normalizes signals from 150+ strategy configurations
requirements.description: The system must aggregate signals from 150+ strategy configurations managed by the Strategy Builder, normalizing them into a common Signal format for ITM consumption. Each signal carries direction, confidence (0-1), source strategy id, and timestamp. Signal aggregation supports deterministic conflict resolution when multiple strategies emit opposing signals.

requirements.title: TradeRegistry maintains central ledger for all optimizer-produced trade records
requirements.description: TradeRegistry is the single source of truth for trade records produced by the optimizer. Schema or logic changes can silently corrupt trade history and backtest replay. Registry must be scoped to a single backtest run (no global mutable state across runs).

requirements.title: ValidationReportWindow displays institutional-grade validation results with auto-fix actions
requirements.description: The ValidationReportWindow is the 2300-line institutional-grade report UI displaying validation results with auto-fix actions. It is the final gate before strategy submission. Must correctly render all validation issues, auto-fix options, and submission controls.
